column8: Carrie and her mum were looking at old photos of her mum as a university student. Her mum's style was so different then, Carrie almost didn't___________her in the photos.
column9: "I can't believe how long your hair is in the picture, Mum!" Carrie remarked, having never seen her mum with that length of hair before. "It must have been difficult to look_______________it every day."
column10: Carrie's mum smiled at her remarks. "It was definitely a chore, but that was the style back then, to have really long, straight hair__________down the middle. If you didn't wear your hair like that, you weren't considered to be cool, know what I mean?"
column11: "Oh, yeah, like groovy, Mum," Carrie said, lightly poking fun________________her, Joking aside, Carrie really appreciated seeing her mum with such a youthful face.
column12: They found some photos of Carrie's dad. He was sporting a full head of hair, rather than having the____________head that Carrie knew.
column13: "Dad was so handsome back than", Carrie exclaimed. Despite the odd styles, Carrie thought her parents looked amazing and she was inspired__________this to be more daring with her own style.
column14: After viewing the photos, Carrie though she'd_____________some time in picking out a new wardrobe. She wasn't going to let her conservative ways stop her.
